hypothesis: If an LLM-driven agent is augmented with domain tools (memory, periodic table, knowledge base) and closed-loop structure-property feedback (DiffCSP + iComFormer), then it will generate valid, unique, and novel inorganic materials that meet specified formation-energy targets and natural-language constraints at higher success rates than tool-free LLMs or diffusion-only baselines like MatterGen because tool-guided retrieval and iterative feedback direct proposals toward thermodynamically favorable regions.
